simpleExplanation.title: Market analysis — explained simply
simpleExplanation.summary: Think of market analysis as one building block in your Paper 2 (Structured Questions) toolkit. Examiners rarely ask for memorised paragraphs — they want you to apply the idea in a structured answer.
simpleExplanation.analogy: If the full lesson feels dense, focus on one past-paper question: read the mark scheme after attempting it — that shows what Cambridge actually rewards.

faq.q: Is this Economics Market analysis lesson free?
faq.a: Yes — every topic in our 2281 course is free. MarkScheme offers free past-paper marking so you can turn notes into marks.
Scroll to the past-paper section on this page — we surface real Cambridge questions tagged to 3.9 from our mark-scheme library.

sections.content: Welcome to your **free Economics** lesson on **Market analysis** (3.9). This is syllabus point 3.9 on **Paper 2 (Structured Questions)** — structured like a premium revision course, with past-paper practice built in.

items: Master the vocabulary examiners use in mark schemes for Market analysis
Know which paper (P2) tests this topic most often
Practise at least one official past-paper question after reading
Use “Explain simpler” below if the main lesson feels too advanced

sections.content: Cambridge 2281 rewards clear definitions, labelled diagrams, and applied examples from the data. On Paper 2 (Structured Questions), Market analysis often appears as MC items or structured questions — plan before writing longer answers.
